subsection.chapeau: The Board may prescribe the form of records required to be prepared or compiled under this subchapter—

paragraph.content: by rail carriers and lessors, including records related to movement of traffic and receipts and expenditures of money; and
by persons furnishing cars to or for a rail carrier providing transportation subject to the jurisdiction of the Board under this part to the extent related to those cars or that service.

subsection.chapeau: The Board, or an employee designated by the Board, may on demand and display of proper credentials—

paragraph.content: inspect and examine the lands, buildings, and equipment of a rail carrier or lessor; and

paragraph.chapeau: inspect and copy any record of—

subparagraph.content: a rail carrier, lessor, or association;
a person controlling, controlled by, or under common control with a rail carrier if the Board considers inspection relevant to that person’s relation to, or transaction with, that rail carrier; and
a person furnishing cars to or for a rail carrier if the Board prescribed the form of that record.

subsection.content: The Board may prescribe the time period during which operating, accounting, and financial records must be preserved by rail carriers, lessors, and persons furnishing cars.

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 11144, Pub. L. 95–473, Oct. 17, 1978, 92 Stat. 1426; Pub. L. 96–296, § 24(c), July 1, 1980, 94 Stat. 816, related to form, inspection, and preservation of records, prior to the general amendment of this subtitle by Pub. L. 104–88, § 102(a). See sections 11144, 14122, and 15722 of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective Jan. 1, 1996, except as otherwise provided in Pub. L. 104–88, see section 2 of Pub. L. 104–88, set out as a note under section 1301 of this title.
